articles.title: Voxtral TTS
articles.summary: Introduces Voxtral TTS, an expressive multilingual text-to-speech model that generates natural speech from just 3 seconds of reference audio. The model uses a hybrid architecture combining auto-regressive semantic token generation with flow-matching for acoustic tokens.

articles.title: Voxtral Realtime
articles.summary: Presents Voxtral Realtime, a natively streaming automatic speech recognition model that matches offline transcription quality at sub-second latency. Unlike approaches that adapt offline models, this is trained end-to-end for streaming with explicit audio-text alignment.

articles.title: Self-Improving Pretraining: using post-trained models to pretrain better models
articles.summary: Proposes a paradigm shift where post-trained models are used to improve pretraining data quality. This approach addresses the limitation that desirable behaviors like safety and reasoning are only added in late-stage training.

articles.title: Flow Map Language Models: One-step Language Modeling via Continuous Denoising
articles.summary: Demonstrates that language models based on continuous flows over one-hot token embeddings can outperform discrete diffusion models. This approach enables faster generation than autoregressive models while maintaining quality.

articles.title: Projected Autoregression: Autoregressive Language Generation in Continuous State Space
articles.summary: Replaces discrete token selection with continuous prediction in embedding space followed by discrete projection. This approach allows for more flexible autoregressive generation while maintaining compatibility with existing architectures.

articles.title: GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion
articles.summary: GitHub introduces Rubber Duck feature that provides different perspectives to Copilot CLI by combining multiple model families. This gives developers a second opinion on their code solutions.

articles.title: ENCRUST: Encapsulated Substitution and Agentic Refinement on a Live Scaffold for Safe C-to-Rust Translation
articles.summary: Presents a two-phase pipeline for translating real-world C projects to safe Rust. The approach handles cross-unit type mismatches and unsafe constructs requiring whole-program reasoning.

articles.title: Inside the Scaffold: A Source-Code Taxonomy of Coding Agent Architectures
articles.summary: Provides a systematic analysis of LLM-based coding agent architectures by examining the scaffolding code that surrounds language models. The study identifies architectural patterns in control loops, tool definitions, and context strategies.

articles.title: Collective AI can amplify tiny perturbations into divergent decisions
articles.summary: Shows that iterative multi-LLM deliberation can amplify tiny perturbations into divergent conversational trajectories and different final decisions. Even in fully deterministic benchmarks, exact reruns can lead to dramatically different outcomes.

articles.title: Truth as a Compression Artifact in Language Model Training
articles.summary: Shows that language models trained on contradictory data prefer correct answers based on compressibility structure rather than truth itself. Controlled experiments reveal that preference tracks error compressibility patterns.

articles.title: LLMs Encode Their Failures: Predicting Success from Pre-Generation Activations
articles.summary: Demonstrates that linear probes on pre-generation activations can predict task success before text generation begins. This enables more efficient inference by selectively applying expensive reasoning only when needed.

articles.title: Hallucination Basins: A Dynamic Framework for Understanding and Controlling LLM Hallucinations
articles.summary: Presents a geometric dynamical systems framework where hallucinations arise from task-dependent basin structure in latent space. The research shows that separability is strongly task-dependent rather than universal.

articles.title: When Do Hallucinations Arise? A Graph Perspective on the Evolution of Path Reuse and Path Compression
articles.summary: Models next-token prediction as graph search and identifies mechanisms by which decoder-only transformers produce hallucinations. The study reveals how entities and transitions affect reasoning quality.

articles.title: 4 days left to save close to $500 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes
articles.summary: TechCrunch announces final days for early bird pricing on Disrupt 2026 tickets. Attendees can save up to $482 before the April 10 deadline.

articles.title: Paper Espresso: From Paper Overload to Research Insight
articles.summary: Introduces an open-source platform that automatically discovers, summarizes, and analyzes trending arXiv papers using LLMs. The system provides structured summaries and multi-granularity trend analysis.
